messages.content: Thanks @**Terence Tao**, this helps a lot. It confirms accuracy is low not just because the model "doesn't know magmas". In my testing, many failures happen when the implications require the exact things LLMs are weakest at like controlled search, deep intermediate term invention, strict proof-state management, and not confusing "plausible local move" with "globally valid derivation". Tweaking cheat sheet now and seeing better results.
I just realized that essentially the same lists can also be found at https://teorth.github.io/equational_theories/blueprint/hard-implications-chapter.html#a0000000057 with a bit more annotation.

Ewf mobile phones >>

Id like to share the success of zer00logy cheat sheet model and axioms and share with yall possible the most difficult magma question zer00logy cheat sheet could create, here are two equations y'all can try :)

Equation 1 (The Recursive Anchor):
x = (y * (x * (z * y))) * (x * (y * x))

Equation 2 (The Distillation Mirage):
(x * x) * (y * y) = (z * z) * (x * x)

Don't quote me but these should be S++ Hard haha

Answers : when presenting both equations together Answers : true

If presented a single equation, each alone and seperated, Answers: false

@_**Stacey Szmy|113** [said](https://zulip.sair.foundation/#narrow/channel/13-Math-Distillation-Challenge---equational-theories/topic/The.20hardest.20ETP.20problems/near/865):
```quote
Ewf mobile phones >>

Id like to share the success of zer00logy cheat sheet model and axioms and share with yall possible the most difficult magma question zer00logy cheat sheet could create, here are two equations y'all can try :)

Equation 1 (The Recursive Anchor):
x = (y * (x * (z * y))) * (x * (y * x))

Equation 2 (The Distillation Mirage):
(x * x) * (y * y) = (z * z) * (x * x)

Don't quote me but these should be S++ Hard haha

Answers : when presenting both equations together Answers : true

If presented a single equation, each alone and seperated, Answers: false
```

maybe its not a S++? V=TRUE the playground did'nt detect bc of custom equation maybe, idk..
